evals.prompt: Design the architecture for a real-time collaboration tool (like Notion). It needs document editing, user presence, comments, and version history. We have a team of 4 developers and want to launch in 3 months. Should we go monolith or microservices?
evals.expected_output: Architecture recommendation (monolith for small team + tight timeline), module boundaries, data flow, Mermaid diagrams, ADR for the decision

assertions.name: recommends_monolith
assertions.description: Recommends monolith or modular monolith given team size of 4 and 3-month timeline

assertions.name: module_boundaries
assertions.description: Defines module boundaries (documents, collaboration, auth, versioning)

assertions.name: data_flow_diagram
assertions.description: Shows request lifecycle through the system

assertions.name: mermaid_diagrams
assertions.description: Includes at least one Mermaid architecture or sequence diagram

assertions.name: adr_documented
assertions.description: Architecture Decision Record with rationale for pattern choice

assertions.name: addresses_realtime
assertions.description: Addresses WebSocket/SSE strategy for real-time collaboration

evals.prompt: We have a monolith Rails app with 200K requests/day. The search feature is slow and we want to add AI-powered recommendations. Should we extract these as microservices? We have 12 developers across 3 teams.
evals.expected_output: Selective extraction plan — search and AI as separate services, keep the rest monolithic, with communication patterns and migration strategy

assertions.name: selective_extraction
assertions.description: Recommends extracting search and AI only, not breaking up the entire monolith

assertions.name: communication_pattern
assertions.description: Defines how Rails app communicates with new services (REST, gRPC, events)

assertions.name: migration_strategy
assertions.description: Phased migration plan, not big-bang rewrite

assertions.name: team_alignment
assertions.description: Considers 3-team structure in service ownership

assertions.name: infrastructure_diagram
assertions.description: Topology showing monolith + extracted services + databases

evals.prompt: Design the system architecture for a multi-tenant SaaS analytics platform. It needs to ingest event data (100K events/sec peak), store it for querying, provide real-time dashboards, and support tenant isolation. Budget is moderate — we can't use expensive managed services for everything.
evals.expected_output: Event ingestion pipeline, storage strategy, query layer, tenant isolation approach, infrastructure topology with cost-conscious choices

assertions.name: ingestion_pipeline
assertions.description: Event ingestion design handling 100K events/sec (queue + workers pattern)

assertions.name: storage_strategy
assertions.description: Appropriate storage for analytics (ClickHouse, TimescaleDB, or similar OLAP)

assertions.name: tenant_isolation
assertions.description: Tenant isolation strategy (row-level, schema-level, or database-level)

assertions.name: cost_conscious
assertions.description: Makes cost-appropriate choices, not just defaulting to most expensive managed services

assertions.name: mermaid_diagrams
assertions.description: Architecture and data flow diagrams included

evals.prompt: We're building a mobile app (React Native) with a NestJS backend. The app needs offline support for viewing data, background sync when connectivity returns, and push notifications. Design the overall architecture.
evals.expected_output: Architecture covering mobile offline storage, sync protocol, push notification flow, API design for offline-first, and conflict resolution strategy

assertions.name: offline_architecture
assertions.description: Addresses local storage on mobile for offline data access

assertions.name: sync_protocol
assertions.description: Defines sync strategy (last-write-wins, CRDT, or custom merge)

assertions.name: push_notification_flow
assertions.description: Shows notification flow from backend event to mobile device

assertions.name: api_design_for_offline
assertions.description: API supports delta sync or pagination for efficient reconnect

assertions.name: conflict_resolution
assertions.description: Addresses what happens when offline edits conflict with server state

evals.prompt: Design a serverless architecture for a document processing pipeline. Users upload PDFs, we extract text (OCR), classify the document type, extract key fields, and store results. Processing should be async. We're on AWS.
evals.expected_output: Serverless pipeline with S3 trigger, Lambda/Step Functions, queue-based processing, and results storage

assertions.name: serverless_pattern
assertions.description: Uses Lambda, Step Functions, or similar serverless compute

assertions.name: event_driven
assertions.description: S3 upload triggers the processing pipeline

assertions.name: async_processing
assertions.description: Processing is async — user gets a job ID, not a synchronous result

assertions.name: step_function_or_queue
assertions.description: Multi-step pipeline using Step Functions or SQS queue chain

assertions.name: results_storage
assertions.description: Processed results stored in DynamoDB, RDS, or S3
